coverage.surface: the 3 M242 UX changes end-to-end: (1) tuple-regrouped content rows (group by (sim_type,modality) | label fallback; passed|not-passed columns; symmetric empty markers; presence-only inline) (2) header-resident tab selector (flex .hwrap with content, byte-identical else-branch without) (3) role-tinted hero avatars (manager orange / employee indigo / candidate teal, manager-wins, AA-contrast). Plus the close fix: the empty-column marker maintained under the M241 EN/IT toggle (_LANG_JS.syncEmpty)
coverage.residual_uncovered: the live click behaviour of _LANG_JS.syncEmpty (per-column marker inject on toggle) is pure DOM, proven structurally + by render-shape here; the live browser proof rides M244's e2e execution (the language-toggle spec is already in stack-verify/e2e/, unexecuted this release). The 6 pre-existing academy+overlay cockpit failures -> M244

notes: Fifth of the v2.6 post-barrier fix fan-out (cockpit UX), serial after M241 (wanted the pass/fail + language variants). A clean complete 3-section close — render/CSS only in the presenter cockpit, no data/seed change, no manifest schema change, 0 platform-repo edits. Harden found + fixed 2 toothless tests + added a WCAG contrast pin; the close's adversarial pass found + landed 1 latent D3-invariant gap (the empty column under the language toggle). All assertions are STRUCTURE / PRESENCE / class-placement, never manifest copy. The rext consumption tag was re-pinned twice this milestone: first at ACTION-1 to the harden HEAD (b0e76b3), then at close to the close-fix HEAD (73d37d5, the final code-of-record).
